blocks:
Surah Al-Balad

Meccan, and it consists of twenty verses without dispute. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) لا أُقْسِمُ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ. (1) I swear by this city, (٢)** وَأَنْتَ حِلٌّ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ ** (2) while you are a sojourner in this city— It is said: meaning, I swear by this Sacred City, meaning Mecca, due to the honor of the one who resides therein, and He is the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله.

In al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Quraysh used to venerate the City but deemed Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله lawful (to harm) therein. So Allah said: لا أُقْسِمُ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ وَأَنْتَ حِلٌّ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ ** (I swear by this city, while you are a sojourner in this city—). He means that they deemed you lawful (to harm) therein, so they belied you and insulted you. And a man among them would not seize the killer of his father therein (in the City), and they would wear the bark of a tree from the Sacred Precinct (الحرم) and feel safe by wearing it. Yet they deemed lawful concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله what they did not deem lawful concerning others. So Allah censured that upon them.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): what is close to it (the previous meaning). And al-Qummi (said): الْبَلَدِ ** (the City) is Mecca. وَأَنْتَ حِلٌّ بِهذَا الْبَلَدِ ** (while you are a sojourner in this city—). He said: Quraysh did not deem it lawful to oppress anyone in this City, yet they deemed it lawful to oppress you therein.

** وَوالِدٍ وَما وَلَدَ.** (3) and the father and what he begot.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means Adam (peace be upon him), and وَما وَلَدَ ** (and what he begot) refers to the Prophets and the Successors (الأوصياء), peace be upon them, and their followers (أتباعهم).

And al-Qummi: the like of it.

In al-Kafi, narrated مرفوعاً (with an elevated chain), the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: And those who were begotten from the Imams (الأئمّة), peace be upon them.

(٤) لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ فِي كَبَدٍ ** (4) We have certainly created man in travail. It was said: meaning, in toil (تعب) and hardship (مشقّة), for he endures the calamities of the world and the severities of the Hereafter. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, upright (منتصباً). And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "We see two patches on the palms of the hands of the beasts (الدوابّ), like cauterization (الكيّ). From what thing is that?" So He said: "That is the place of its nostrils (منخريه) in the womb of its mother. And the son of Adam, his head is upright (منتصب) in the womb of his mother. And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ فِي كَبَدٍ ** And whatever is other than the son of Adam, its head is in its rear (دبره) and its two hands are in front of it."

(٥) أَيَحْسَبُ أَنْ لَنْ يَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَحَدٌ ** (5) Does he reckon that no one will ever overpower him? so that He may take vengeance upon him. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: It means that he will be killed for his killing of the daughter of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. I say: Perhaps the third one (الثّالث) is intended by this. (٦) يَقُولُ أَهْلَكْتُ مالاً لُبَداً ** (6) He says, ‘I have squandered a massive wealth!’ much (كثيراً), derived from the matting (تلبّد) of a thing when it gathers. Al-Qummi (said) لبداً means, i.e., gathered (مجتمعاً). And in the previous narration, He said: It means that which he equipped the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with during the Army of Hardship (جيش العسرة). And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: It is Amr ibn Abd Wudd, when Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) presented Islam to him on the Day of the Trench (يوم الخندق), and he said: "Then where is what I spent among you, **مالاً لُبَداً ** (a massive wealth)?" And he had spent wealth in hindering [others] from the path of Allah, so Ali (peace be upon him) killed him.

(٧) أَيَحْسَبُ أَنْ لَمْ يَرَهُ أَحَدٌ. (7) Does he reckon that no one has seen him? Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] the corruption (الفساد) that was within his soul (نفسه). (٨) أَلَمْ نَجْعَلْ لَهُ عَيْنَيْنِ ** (8) Did We not make for him two eyes, with which he sees. (٩) وَلِساناً ** (9) and a tongue, with which he expresses (يترجم) what is in his conscience (ضمائره) وَشَفَتَيْنِ ** and two lips, with which He covers his mouth (فاه) and seeks assistance for speaking (النّطق), eating (الاكل), drinking (الشرب), and other things. (١٠) وَهَدَيْناهُ النَّجْدَيْنِ. (10) and guided him to the two highways? In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: The highway (نجد) of good (الخير) and [the highway] of evil (الشرّ). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The path (سبيل) of good (الخير) and the path (سبيل) of evil (الشرّ). And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): It was said to Him that some people say regarding His saying: وَهَدَيْناهُ النَّجْدَيْنِ ** (and guided him to the two highways?) that they are the two breasts (الثديان). So He said: "No, they are good (الخير) and evil (الشرّ)."

(١١) فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ الْعَقَبَةَ ** (So he did not brave the uphill road.) That is, he did not thank those favors (الايادي) by braving the uphill road (الْعَقَبَةَ), which is entering into a severe matter. It was said that العقبة is the path in the mountain. He used it metaphorically for what He explained it with, namely, the freeing (الفكّ) and the feeding (الإطعام). And Al-Qummi said: الْعَقَبَةَ is the Imams (الأئمّة); whoever ascends it frees his neck from the Fire.

(١٢) وَما أَدْراكَ مَا الْعَقَبَةُ. (12) And what will show you what the steep pass is? (١٣) فَكُّ رَقَبَةٍ. (13) The freeing of a slave, (١٤) أَوْ إِطْعامٌ فِي يَوْمٍ ذِي مَسْغَبَةٍ ** (14) or feeding on a day of starvation, meaning, possessing hunger (مجاعة). (١٥) يَتِيماً ذا مَقْرَبَةٍ ** (15) an orphan having kinship, meaning, possessing kinship (قرابة). (١٦) أَوْ مِسْكِيناً ذا مَتْرَبَةٍ ** (16) or a needy person lying in the dust. meaning, possessing poverty (فقر). Al-Qummi said: Nothing protects him from the dust. And it was recited: فكّ رقبة أو اطعم (The freeing of a slave or feed).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): When He (peace be upon him) ate, a plate (صحفة) would be brought and placed near His dining cloth (مائدة). He would then proceed to the best of the food that was brought, taking a portion of everything, and placing it in that plate. Then He would order it to be given to the needy (المساكين). Then He would recite this Ayah: **فَلَا اقْتَحَمَ ** (But he did not brave the steep pass). Then He would say: "Allah knew that not every person is able to free a slave, so He made for them the path to Paradise."

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever feeds a believer until he is satisfied, none of Allah's creation knows what reward he has in the Hereafter—neither an angel brought near (ملك مقرّب) nor a sent Prophet (نبيّ مرسل)—except Allah, the Lord of the worlds." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Among the causes that necessitate forgiveness is feeding the hungry Muslim (المسلم السّغبان)." Then He recited: **أَوْ إِطْعامٌ ** the Ayah.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "Whoever Allah has honored with our Wilayah (ولايتنا) has indeed passed الْعَقَبَةَ ** (the steep pass). And we are that steep pass (العقبة) which whoever braves it is saved." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "All people are slaves of the Fire except you and your companions, for Allah has freed your necks (رقابكم) from the Fire through our Wilayah (ولايتنا), the People of the House (أهل البيت)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Through us are the necks (الرقاب) freed, and through our knowledge (معرفتنا). And we are those who feed on the day of hunger, which is al-Masghabah (المسغبة)."

(١٧) ثُمَّ كانَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَتَواصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ وَتَواصَوْا بِالْمَرْحَمَةِ. (17) Then he is among those who have faith, and enjoin one another to patience, and enjoin one another to compassion. (١٨) أولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْمَيْمَنَةِ ** (18) They are the People of the Right Hand. al-Qummi said: They are the companions (أصحاب) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). (١٩) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِنا ** (19) But those who defy Our signs— He said: They are those who opposed the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) هُمْ أَصْحابُ الْمَشْأَمَةِ they are the People of the Left Hand. He said: The People of the Left Hand (أصحاب المشأمة) are the enemies of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). (٢٠) عَلَيْهِمْ نارٌ مُؤْصَدَةٌ ** (20) upon them shall be a fire sealed off. He said: meaning, sealed (مُطْبَقَة).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites لا اقْسِمُ بهذا البلد (I swear by this city) in an obligatory prayer (فريضة), he will be known in this world as one of the righteous (الصالحين), and it will be known in the Hereafter that he has a station, and on the Day of Resurrection, he will be among the companions (رفقاء) of the Prophets (peace be upon them), the martyrs (الشهداء), and the righteous (الصالحين), if Allah the Almighty wills."
